title: Poikilospermum hirsutum
taxonomy: Poikilospermum hirsutum is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Poikilospermum, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The genus Poikilospermum is part of the order Malpighiales, which includes many families of flowering plants. Poikilospermum hirsutum is also known as hairy polyspermum or hairy poikilospermum. This plant is commonly found in tropical forests and is known for its distinctive hairy leaves.
uses: Poikilospermum hirsutum is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. Research on the plant has indicated its potential medicinal uses, including the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties.
